personal.name: Qatada
personal.title: PHP & Module Developer · Full Stack Engineer · CS Student
taglines: From university benches to production servers.
personal.bio: I'm a 6th-semester Computer Science student at FAST-NUCES Lahore, currently working part-time as a PHP & Module Developer at WHMPRESS. I've been writing code since my first semester and haven't stopped — from terminal-based C++ games to AI-powered chat platforms with WhatsApp agent integration. I care deeply about clean architecture, great UX, and shipping things that actually work.
personal.shortBio: CS student at FAST-NUCES · PHP Dev at WHMPRESS · Ex-intern & freelancer. I build full-stack products — fast.
personal.workStatus: Part-time at WHMPRESS · Open to freelance

education.fullName: National University of Computer and Emerging Sciences
education.degree: Bachelor of Science in Computer Science
highlights: Relevant coursework: AI, Computer Architecture, Data Structures, OOP, Databases, Networks, OS
Built 5+ full-stack academic projects across semesters
Active member of the developer community on campus
Maintained part-time work alongside full academic load from Semester 5
Completed A-Levels with focus on Mathematics, Physics, and Computer Science
Developed early interest in programming and software systems

experience.description: Working part-time as a PHP and module developer while completing my degree. Developing and maintaining WHMCS addon modules and integrations, including billing automation, promo tracking, and URL management systems.
responsibilities: Developing custom WHMCS addon modules from scratch using PHP
Building WHMPress Promo & URL Tracking module with full CRUD admin panel
Writing maintainable, modular PHP following WHMCS hook/API patterns
Collaborating with product team to scope and spec new module features
Testing and debugging production-level module integrations

experience.description: Summer internship building internal tools and dashboards. Worked across the full stack — from Python/FastAPI backends to React frontends — with AI integrations using the OpenAI API.
responsibilities: Built a Report Automation system that reduced manual reporting time by 80%
Developed Work Progress Dashboards for real-time project tracking
Created an Office Inventory Management system with role-based access
Integrated OpenAI API for intelligent data extraction and summarisation
Deployed and managed services using Node.js and FastAPI backends

experience.description: Took on freelance contracts during Semester 5 alongside university coursework. Delivered web development projects for international clients on the Upwork platform.
responsibilities: Delivered full-stack web applications for international clients
Built custom React components and responsive layouts
Integrated third-party APIs and backend services
Maintained client communication and met strict delivery deadlines
Managed multiple simultaneous contracts alongside university coursework

projects.name: NeuraChat
projects.tagline: AI-powered chat platform with WhatsApp agent integration
projects.description: A full-featured AI chat platform built in Semester 5. Features real-time messaging, LangChain-powered AI agents, and a WhatsApp integration that allows the AI to send and receive messages autonomously via a connected WhatsApp account.
highlights: Real-time chat with WebSocket (Socket.io) architecture
LangChain agent with tool-calling for WhatsApp automation
Multi-session support with persistent chat history
Deployed on Vercel with NestJS backend

projects.name: SkilLink
projects.description: A fully functional freelance marketplace built in Semester 4. Features user authentication, project listings, bidding system, real-time chat, and a client/freelancer dashboard — modelled closely after Upwork.
highlights: Role-based auth: client vs freelancer dashboards
Project posting, bidding, and contract management
Real-time messaging between clients and freelancers
Full CRUD with REST API backend

projects.name: Flappy Bird (Assembly)
projects.tagline: Classic arcade game in x86 Assembly (COAL)
projects.description: A fully playable Flappy Bird clone written in x86 Assembly language (COAL) in Semester 3. Rendered using DOS video memory with collision detection and score tracking — all in assembly.
highlights: Direct video memory manipulation for rendering
Collision detection in pure assembly
Score tracking and game loop in assembly
Runs in DOSBox emulator

projects.name: Tetris
projects.description: The classic Tetris game built in C++ during Semester 1 as a first major project. Runs in the terminal with real-time input handling, piece rotation, line clearing, and score tracking.
highlights: Real-time keyboard input without blocking
All 7 Tetromino shapes with rotation logic
Score system and increasing difficulty
Written from scratch, no game libraries
